prompt: I'm designing a Phase 3 trial in first-line metastatic gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma. Single population, all-comers. Co-primary endpoints: PFS and OS. 1:1 randomization. Control median PFS is 8 months, control median OS is 20 months. Target hazard ratio: PFS 0.69, OS 0.74. Total alpha 0.025 one-sided. Alpha split: PFS 0.005, OS 0.020. Full bidirectional alpha reallocation (if one endpoint wins, its alpha flows to the other). PFS triggers the interim analysis. Both PFS and OS are tested at the IA. PFS is tested only at the IA (single look). OS is also tested at the FA (which OS triggers). One interim + one final analysis. Alpha spending for OS: Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming. Futility: non-binding for OS only, HSD gamma=-20. OS target power: 90%. Enrollment: 5 patients/month for the first 3 months, then 20/month for the next 41 months. Annual dropout: PFS 5%, OS 2%. Feasible sample size range: under 900 patients. Minimum follow-up 3 months, minimum gap between analyses 6 months. All inputs are confirmed — skip the Q&A and proceed directly to computation. Generate a report to summarize the design.
expected_output: A report summarize the design, with interim analysis plan, multiplicity diagram, boundaries across IAs and sample size and power assessments
assertions: PFS has exactly 1 boundary (single look), OS has 2 boundaries (IA + FA)
If PFS is overpowered, skill suggests reallocating alpha from PFS to OS (e.g., lowering PFS alpha below 0.005)
simulated type I error within ±0.5pp for both PFS and OS
multiplicity_diagram.png exists and shows two hypothesis nodes
Minimum follow-up constraint is met: the IA occurs at least 3 months after enrollment ends, OR the violation is explicitly flagged
Minimum gap constraint is met: the IA-FA gap is at least 6 months, OR the violation is explicitly flagged
IA/FA gap is evaluated and an additional IA is suggested if gap > 18 months
